delta_scope: commits 3bbdf86 (LiveLedgerBridgeConsumer + main.ts wiring) + c836011 (live-ledger-wiring.e2e.test.ts TW1-TW4). ORCH-LV-H1 fix reviewed. Prior FULL PASS surfaces not re-run.

findings.title: acquireRepullLock commits before work begins — row lock released before re-pull executes
findings.detail: FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED lock acquired then committed before the re-pull page loop starts. Narrow window between COMMIT and setSyncState('syncing') where two concurrent workers could both proceed. Data correctness protected by Bronze event_id dedup (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) and ledger idempotency. No security breach or data-integrity violation — worst-case is double Shopify API calls. Remediate by holding lock open for full re-pull duration or adding status='syncing' pre-check.

findings.title: Non-null assertion on updatedAt — NaN date if order has no timestamp fields
findings.detail: If order.updated_at ?? order.processed_at ?? order.created_at is undefined, non-null assertion causes NaN to be passed to new Date().getTime(). Shopify always provides updated_at on order webhooks so this is a theoretical edge case. Remediate: add explicit null guard (if !updatedAt -> discard with 200, same as order.id type check at line 186).

delta_gates.guc_before_ledger_write: PASS — LedgerWriter.writeProvisionalRecognition:83-85 and writeReversal:182-184 both call set_config('app.current_brand_id', $1, true) as the first statement inside BEGIN, before any INSERT. brandId sourced from parsed envelope brand_id field (string type-check at LiveLedgerBridgeConsumer.ts:82), never from env/header.
delta_gates.brand_id_derivation_safe: PASS — brand_id extracted from Kafka message body via typeof check (string guard at line 82). The message is already on the live topic which was routed there by CollectorEventConsumer after HMAC-first webhook validation + brand-from-DB anti-spoof (D-4, verified in FULL review). Cross-tenant spoofing on the topic requires compromising the broker, which is outside threat model.
delta_gates.append_only_no_update_delete: PASS — Both LedgerWriter methods use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (brand_id, order_id, event_type, date) DO NOTHING RETURNING. No UPDATE, no DELETE. brain_app grant is SELECT+INSERT only (confirmed migration 0018 in FULL review). writeReversal writes a new negative row; prior rows untouched.
delta_gates.no_double_bronze_write: PASS — LiveLedgerBridgeConsumer calls routeLiveOrderToLedger() (LiveOrderConsumer.ts:89-120) which calls LedgerWriter only. It does not call ProcessEventUseCase, BronzeRepository, or CollectorEventConsumer. Bronze writes remain solely in CollectorEventConsumer (group: stream-worker-live). Confirmed by code inspection: no BronzeRepository import in LiveLedgerBridgeConsumer.ts or LiveOrderConsumer.ts.
delta_gates.filter_cross_tenant_safe: PASS — event_name filter at line 97 checks for exact string 'order.live.v1'. brand_id is taken from the event's own brand_id field which was set by the webhook handler from DB lookup (not from any user-controlled field at emission time). A message with event_name='order.live.v1' and brand_id belonging to Brand A cannot be processed as Brand B because the GUC is set from the same event's brand_id field — cross-tenant confusion requires a message with a forged brand_id, which is precluded by the upstream HMAC + DB lookup chain.
delta_gates.no_new_migration_rls_grant: PASS — commits 3bbdf86 and c836011 contain no .sql files, no migration files, no RLS policy changes, no GRANT statements. Confirmed by file listing: only LiveLedgerBridgeConsumer.ts, main.ts, live-ledger-wiring.e2e.test.ts touched.
delta_gates.no_pii_in_logs: PASS — LiveLedgerBridgeConsumer logs at lines 119-121 (info) and 127-130 (error) log only: brand (UUID), event (UUID), partition (int), offset (int), attempt count, error message string. No email, phone, name, or raw order payload. Error path (line 128-130) logs err which is a DB/Kafka error object — no user PII in those paths.
delta_gates.prior_pass_surfaces_not_regressed: PASS — diff scope (3bbdf86+c836011) touches only: LiveLedgerBridgeConsumer.ts (new file), main.ts (import + 3 wiring lines + shutdown extension), live-ledger-wiring.e2e.test.ts (new test file). HMAC handler, ShopifyHmac.ts, migration 0026, shopify-repull/run.ts, LedgerWriter.ts, LiveOrderConsumer.ts, shopify-mapper, BackfillOrderConsumer — all untouched.
delta_gates.verification_validity: PASS — TW1 and TW2 produce real Kafka messages and poll the ledger under brain_app+GUC. Un-wire proof documented at test line 223: commenting out await consumer.start() causes TW1/TW2 poll to timeout (RED). TW3 negative control: non-order event produces no ledger row (5s wait). TW4 idempotency: exact row count=1 after double-produce + 3s extra wait. All probes non-inert; no bypass-green.
